puzzle.title: The Silent Protector's Burden
puzzle.intro: In a time of great oppression, a divine command brought both profound fear and unwavering hope to a particular soul. This individual faced an unimaginable trial, entrusted with a precious life amidst a ruler's cruel decree. Their story is a testament to trust in the unseen and the miraculous safeguarding of Allah.
clues: I lived in a land where a powerful ruler caused great corruption and tyranny.
I was overcome with fear for my newborn, as the ruler decreed the killing of all male infants.
I received a divine inspiration, a command that seemed both terrifying and miraculous.
I placed my infant in a chest, entrusting him to the currents of a mighty river.
I instructed another of my children to follow the chest's journey, to observe its fate from a distance.
My child was eventually returned to me, to be nursed and raised in the very household that sought his destruction.

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Placing an infant in a river
Leading a migration across a desert
trial.answer: Placing an infant in a river

location.label: Key Element
options: A staff turning into a serpent
A ship built for a flood

outcome.label: Outcome
options: Being reunited with the child for nursing
Witnessing the splitting of the sea
outcome.answer: Being reunited with the child for nursing

puzzle.arabic: وَأَوْحَيْنَآ إِلَىٰٓ أُمِّ مُوسَىٰٓ أَنْ أَرْضِعِيهِ ۖ فَإِذَا خِفْتِ عَلَيْهِ فَأَلْقِيهِ فِى ٱلْيَمِّ وَلَا تَخَافِى وَلَا تَحْزَنِىٓ ۖ إِنَّا رَآدُّوهُ إِلَيْكِ وَجَاعِلُوهُ مِنَ ٱلْمُرْسَلِينَ
puzzle.verse: And We inspired to the mother of Moses, "Suckle him; but when you fear for him, cast him into the river and do not fear and do not grieve. Indeed, We will return him to you and will make him [one] of the messengers." (28:7)
